ops.text: A bare glob-shaped path operand to `ccx code grep` — `ccx code grep escalatable 'internal/ripgrep/*.go'` — passes through to rg unexpanded and surfaces raw rg stderr ("IO error ... No such file or directory") at exit 0's error path, on v0.28.4 and v0.29.0 alike. The documented anchored-glob escape hatch (AGENTS.md entry 4) is the `--glob 'dir/*.go'` shape, which SplitGlobAnchor peels into scope+glob and works fine; bare glob operands were never wired to it. The v0.29.0 lenient path resolver deliberately bypasses metachar-bearing operands, so they still reach the engine raw. Worth wiring glob-bearing path operands through SplitGlobAnchor (or at least a clean error naming the --glob form) — filterGlobPaths already partitions operands against --glob, so the semantics need a design pass, not a drive-by.
